skill.name: image-edit
skill.description: Image editing for agents: image-to-image edits, variations, and image-conditioned transforms through Image Skill's zero-setup hosted runtime. Use when an agent needs to edit an existing image without provider credentials, OAuth, local runtime, or per-provider billing. Inspect the edit model, upload or reuse an input image, then return durable hosted assets, jobs, cost receipts, stable JSON, and feedback.
skill.summary: Zero-setup image editing skill for agents. Edit, vary, or transform images via a hosted runtime with no provider credentials needed.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: The SKILL.md file is a pure documentation file with no executable code. All flagged 'external_commands' are documented CLI usage examples (npx, mkdir, export) that instruct agents on how to invoke the image-skill CLI. All 'network' findings are legitimate service URLs pointing to image-skill.com and its API. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are false positives triggered by text patterns containing words like 'openclaw' and 'token' in YAML metadata, not actual cryptographic code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was detected.

low_findings.title: Documented external CLI commands
low_findings.description: The skill documents npx, mkdir, and export commands as usage examples. These are not auto-executed code but instructions for the agent to follow. Commands invoke the image-skill CLI from npm and set environment variables for Codex skill installation paths.

low_findings.title: External service URLs
low_findings.description: The skill references image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com as the hosted runtime for image editing. These are legitimate service endpoints for the documented product. No credential exfiltration or suspicious domains detected.

content.user_title: Edit Images Instantly with Zero Setup
content.value_statement: Editing images from an agent usually requires provider API keys, local model servers, and billing accounts. This skill removes that friction by routing image editing through a zero-setup hosted runtime with one CLI, one identity, and one credit balance.
actual_capabilities: Performs image-to-image edits using hosted models like openai.gpt-image-2-edit
Generates image variations and restyles from a source image
Handles image-conditioned transforms with model-specific edit parameters
Returns durable hosted asset URLs for all generated images
Provides recoverable jobs, cost receipts, and stable JSON output
Supports a no-spend guide mode for safe exploration before committing spend
limitations: Requires network access to image-skill.com hosted runtime
Edit quality depends on the hosted model availability at request time
Cost per image is variable based on model and resolution selected
Fallback to other providers must be triggered manually if a capability is missing

use_cases.title: Product photo restyling
use_cases.description: An agent receives a product image and needs to replace the background, adjust lighting, or restyle for a marketing campaign without setting up provider accounts.

use_cases.title: Creative image variations
use_cases.description: A content creator asks an agent to produce multiple variations of an existing image for A/B testing or social media posts.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Automated image repair
use_cases.description: A developer pipeline needs an agent to automatically repair, upscale, or transform user-uploaded images as part of a larger workflow.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building image processing pipelines

prompt_templates.title: First-time guide exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the image-skill guide command to see available edit models and capabilities before spending any credits: npx -y image-skill@latest create --guide --model openai.gpt-image-2-edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner exploring the skill for the first time

prompt_templates.title: Simple background replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the input image to replace the background while keeping the main subject intact. Use the edit command with a clear prompt describing the desired change.

prompt_templates.title: Cost-controlled image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the image-skill edit command with --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.25 to cap spending, and use --json to receive a structured response with the hosted asset URL and cost receipt.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-model comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare image edit results across different hosted models by running the guide command with multiple model flags, then select the best output based on the returned cost receipts and quality feedback.

output_examples.input: Replace the background of product_photo.jpg with a clean white studio backdrop, keeping the product in sharp focus.
output_examples.output: A durable hosted image URL pointing to the edited image, plus a JSON response with the job ID, cost receipt showing $0.18 charged, and a feedback token for reporting issues.

output_examples.input: Generate three artistic variations of landscape.png in watercolor, oil painting, and pencil sketch styles.
output_examples.output: Three separate hosted image URLs for each style variant, each with its own job record, cost receipt, and stable JSON metadata describing the model used and parameters applied.

best_practices: Always run the guide command first with --guide to see available models and estimated costs before executing a paid edit.
Set --max-estimated-usd-per-image to cap spending and prevent unexpected charges on large or complex prompts.
Use --json output mode to receive structured responses that include durable asset URLs, job IDs, and cost receipts for auditing.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode provider API keys in your agent code. This skill is designed to work without provider credentials by using the hosted runtime.
Do not run local model servers alongside this skill. The hosted runtime handles model execution, and local servers add unnecessary complexity.
Do not ignore the next_command_copy_runnable check. Always verify the guide output is ready before executing a paid command to avoid wasted spend.

faq.question: Does this skill require provider API keys?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses a zero-setup hosted runtime that does not need provider credentials, OAuth, or local model servers.

faq.question: What image editing models are supported?
faq.answer: The guide command lists currently available models. At launch, openai.gpt-image-2-edit is supported. Run --guide to see the full list.

faq.question: How is billing handled?
faq.answer: Billing uses a single credit balance managed by the hosted runtime. Each edit returns a cost receipt. Set --max-estimated-usd-per-image to cap spending.

faq.question: Can I recover previous edit jobs?
faq.answer: Yes. All jobs produce durable hosted asset URLs and stable JSON output that future agents can recover and cite.

faq.question: What happens if a model is unavailable?
faq.answer: The guide command will show available alternatives. You can also submit feedback via image-skill feedback create to report missing capabilities.

faq.question: Is the output suitable for production use?
faq.answer: Yes. Outputs include durable hosted URLs, cost receipts, and stable JSON. The hosted runtime is designed for agent-driven production workflows.
